decks.notes: This deck focuses on using control tools and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions. Tasigur, the Golden Fang serves as the commander, providing card advantage and a strong body on the battlefield. The deck includes a variety of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to control the game and outvalue the opponent. It also includes some alternate win conditions like Jace, Wielder of Mysteries and Hullbreaker Horror. The mana base is carefully crafted to ensure consistent mana access for casting spells and activating Tasigur's ability. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent while gradually gaining advantage and securing a victory.

This deck focuses on quickly establishing a strong board presence with efficient creatures and disruptive spells to control the game. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd serves as the commander, providing a powerful aggro strategy. The deck includes a variety of cards that protect and support Phelia, such as Mother of Runes and Giver of Runes. It also contains removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with opposing threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Containment Priest. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain control throughout the match to secure a victory.

The primary focus of this deck is to apply early pressure on the opponent through aggressive creature plays while disrupting their game plan with various control elements. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd serves as the commander of this aggro deck, enabling the player to quickly build a board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes a mix of efficient creatures, protective spells, and disruptive cards like Containment Priest, Drannith Magistrate, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Additionally, the deck features strong removal options such as Swords to Plowshares and Skyclave Apparition to deal with threats from the opponent. By combining aggressive threats with disruptive elements, the deck aims to establish dominance on the battlefield and secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize Ral, Monsoon Mage as the commander to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy focused on spell-slinging and generating value through card advantage and tempo plays. The deck includes a variety of cheap cantrips, countermagic, and ritual effects to efficiently cast powerful spells and maintain control of the game. With a mix of card draw, card selection, and mana acceleration, the goal is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells, aiming to burn them out with direct damage or set up a big combo finish. By carefully managing resources and timing spells effectively, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in a 1 vs 1 Duel Commander format.

The main idea of this deck is to ramp up quickly and control the game by generating massive amounts of mana and playing powerful threats. Azusa, Lost but Seeking allows for playing multiple lands per turn, enabling explosive starts. The deck includes various land-based ramp spells like Wild Growth and Burgeoning, as well as big threats like Emrakul, the Promised End and Primeval Titan. It also features interaction with cards like Tectonic Edge and Ghost Quarter to disrupt opponents' strategies. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the game with powerful lands and creatures while overwhelming opponents with mana advantage and potent threats.

The focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's board while steadily building up resources and advantage. The deck includes a range of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, Damnation, Toxic Deluge, and Fatal Push to keep the opponent's creatures in check. Cards like Thoughtseize, Inquisition of Kozilek, and Duress help to strip the opponent's hand of key threats and disrupt their game plan. The deck also has graveyard hate with Leyline of the Void and Rest in Peace, as well as permanent removal with cards like Parallax Wave and Portable Hole. The commander, Ketramose, the New Dawn, provides a strong aggro presence to finish off the opponent once their resources are depleted.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of white, red, and blue cards, utilizing the Partner commanders Kraum and Yoshimaru. It includes a variety of creatures, removal spells, and equipment to quickly overwhelm the opponent and control the board. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain board presence with powerful creatures like Adeline, Kari Zev, and Thalia, while using removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Lightning Bolt to deal with threats. The inclusion of equipment like Embercleave and Shadowspear enhances the deck's combat abilities, allowing for quick and efficient attacks. Overall, the deck aims to be proactive and aggressive, leveraging its mix of creatures, removal, and equipment to dominate the battlefield and secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing Magda, the Hoardmaster's ability to ramp and create treasure tokens to accelerate into powerful aggressive creatures and burn spells. It prioritizes fast mana generation to get ahead on board and disrupt opponents with efficient removal and direct damage spells. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creatures like Grim Lavamancer and Seasoned Pyromancer, as well as burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fiery Confluence. The deck aims to close out games quickly by applying pressure with aggressive threats and finishing off opponents with direct burn damage. It emphasizes proactive plays and efficient use of resources to maintain a strong tempo throughout the game.
